mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 52 percent over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index during late-cycle expansions indicate capacity constraints and margin compression, forcing negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases do not precede negative 20-day returns, or the predictive coefficient flips positive with t-statistic exceeding 2.0.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly boosts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns within the following trading week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation breaching 2.5 percent annualized forces central bank tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative returns over three months.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in 70 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5 percent threshold, or the signal fails to achieve statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting higher equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and UNRATE signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with out-of-sample hit rate falling below 50 percent over consecutive 1-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in real personal spending during post-shock recovery phases signal demand fragility, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines during post-shock recovery periods do not precede negative 5-day returns, or the predictive relationship reverses direction with statistical significance.
